name: Agent Provenance
description: Proof-carrying agent provenance / verifiable memory — a capability-gated, append-only, tamper-evident provenance log (a verifiable agent scratchpad). Each entry is a WriteOnce slot in a blake3 hash chain; the HEAD cursor is Monotonic (append-only). Any party can recompute and VERIFY the chain.
state_constraints: WriteOnce(ENTRY_BASE + i)  // each entry slot frozen forever (tamper-evidence)

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry; DeosApp::mount yields the axum router. register(ctx) calls register_deos(ctx) so the same mount serves the floor factory AND the deos surface.
deos.publish: Signature (the log cell is a dregg:// sturdyref a third-party auditor on another federation reacquires)

affordances.view_provenance: cap-only, Signature (a VERIFIER reads + re-derives the chain — verify_chain's deos home)
affordances.append_entry: GATED (cap∧state), Either — log_initialized precondition (HEAD>=0); the fire reads live HEAD+TIP and submits the FULL append (new entry WriteOnce slot + HEAD advance + TIP + event), the executor re-enforcing Monotonic(HEAD) + WriteOnce(entry). Accumulating: each fire writes the NEXT entry.

deos.seam: CLOSED — the fire runs against the seeded log cell carrying the provenance program (Monotonic(HEAD) + WriteOnce(entries)), so a REWOUND head (Monotonic) and an OVERWRITE of a sealed entry (WriteOnce) BITE in the deos fire path as REAL executor refusals (tests/deos_seam.rs). Monotonic is >= so a no-op (1->1) is allowed; the refusal tooth is a REWIND.
